THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF BIELOUS, MICHAEL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT NGAI, ANTHONY K. $150,000.00 PRINCIPAL, $29,625.00 ATTORNEY FEES, $79,301.12 INTEREST, $847.70 COSTS, $450,000.00 PUNITIVE DAMAGES, TOTAL JUDGMENT $709,773.82, SEE SCANNED DOC - ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT ANTHONY K. NGAI ACTED WITH FRAUD AS TO PLAINTIFF, INCLUDING FRAUDULENT MISREPRESENTATION AND A LACK OF INTENTION OR EXPECTATION TO REPAY PLAINTIFF WHEN THE LOAN WAS MADE.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-22-2019 Off Calendar

Application For Entry Of Court Judgment After Default
Off Calendar Feb-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Anthony K. Nagai.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Randall E. Kay, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Michael Bielous, who was placed under oath and testified.
Inna Ryzhenko, a witness for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Anthony K. Ngai.
Plaintiff's complaint for breach of contract was filed on April 10, 2018.
The Summons, Complaint and Statement of Damages (Punitive) were personally served on defendant Anthony K. Ngai on May 10, 2018.
Default as to defendant Anthony K. Ngai was entered on June 21, 2018.
DOE defendants were dismissed on October 19, 2018.
Statement of Damages was filed on October 19, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Michael Bielous, and against the defaulted defendant, Anthony K. Ngai, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $150,000.00.
Prejudtment Interest - $79,301.12
Attorney Fees - $29,625.00.
Costs - $847.70.
Punitive Damages - $450,000.00.
Total - $709,773.82.

Additionally, the Court finds that defendant Anthony K. Ngai acted with fraud as to plaintiff, including fraudulent misrepresentation and a lack of intention or expectation to repay plaintiff when the loan was made.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Application For Entry Of Court Judgment After Default
Off Calendar Jan-28-2019 Continued To Feb-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered: Ccp Sec. 473(B); Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Anthony K. Ngai, Exhibts A-K, Attached Thereto; Declaration Of Jacqueline M. Phillips, Exhibits 1-4, Attached Thereto
Off Calendar Jan-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Randall E. Kay, Esq. 415-345-1170 for Plaintiff.
Jacqueline Phillips, Esq. 415-595-8956 Specially appearing for Defendant ANTHONY NGAI.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court raises his concerns. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ANTHONY NGAI's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered: Ccp Sec. 473(B).

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Anthony K. Ngai's motion to set aside the default entered on June 21, 2018 is denied. Mr. Ngai has not made a showing that the default was taken against him through his mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. There is undisputed evidence that after he was personally served with the summons and complaint, Mr. Ngai retained counsel, who specifically informed plaintiff's counsel that "Mr. Ngai does not intend to respond to your client's complaint." Further, Mr. Ngai does not deny that in June 2018, he and his counsel requested and received a copy of defendant's request for entry of default. He has not shown that he exercised diligence in seeking relief from default within a reasonable period of time after being served with the request for entry of default. (See Stafford v. Allstate Ins. Co. (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1174, 1183-84 [trial court abused its discretion by granting relief where defendant waited to file its motion until the last day of the six-month limitation period provided by section 473 without an adequate explanation to justify the prolonged delay].)(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
